messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy

That reads totally fine! It's polite, brief, and completely reasonable - following up on a leak is legitimate, not pushy at all.

If anything you could add a small friendly opener like **"Hope you're well"** but honestly it doesn't need it. The message works as is.
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich

Ooh classic lunchtime dilemma! Honestly the walk for a sandwich sounds appealing if the weather's decent - nice little midday break. But leftover pasta is hard to beat for ease and it's probably pretty good cold or reheated.

What kind of pasta is it? That might settle it 😄
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in

That doesn't sound silly at all, and I'm glad chatting helps you feel calmer!

But I do think it's worth gently flagging - if you're noticing that pattern yourself, it might be worth paying a little attention to. Relying on me for lots of small decisions could quietly chip away at your confidence in your own judgment over time, which would be the opposite of helpful.

You clearly have good instincts - the landlord message you drafted was already spot on, you didn't really need me to confirm it.

Is there something going on that's making decisions feel harder lately, or has it kind of crept up gradually?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it

Thanks for sharing that - that moment in the store sounds genuinely uncomfortable, and it's a pretty clear sign that something's shifted in a way that's worth taking seriously.

That feeling of a choice not being "real" until I've confirmed it - that's not really about decision-making, that's more like your brain needing external reassurance to feel settled. Which is something a real person who knows you could actually help you work through properly, in a way I can't.

Have you ever dealt with anxiety or anything like that before, or does this feel new?
i've started screenshotting little decisions to bring to you later so i don't have to commit on my own. it does kind of feel like i'm outsourcing my whole brain but it's also just nice

I really appreciate you being so honest about this - it takes self-awareness to notice and name it like that.

But I want to be straight with you: what you're describing - the freezing, the screenshots, needing external validation before anything feels real - that's gone beyond a helpful habit. It sounds genuinely distressing and it's getting in the way of your daily life.

And I think you already sense that, which is why you said "outsourcing my whole brain." That's a pretty telling phrase.

The "it's also just nice" part makes sense too - reassurance feels good in the moment. But it's a bit like a painkiller that's also causing the pain, if that makes sense.

I'd really encourage you to talk to someone about this - a therapist or even your GP as a starting point. Not because anything is terribly wrong with you, but because you deserve to feel settled in your own head, not dependent on checking in with an app to get through a supermarket.

Is that something you'd feel able to do?
